tests.name: decayFactor — age to decay mapping covers the three boundary cases and the clamp
decayFactor — age to decay mapping linearly interpolates between 1 and 0 across [0, lifetime]
prunePulses — retention-based trim retains fresh pulses and drops pulses older than the cutoff
prunePulses — retention-based trim returns a new array without mutating the input, and is idempotent
buildFrame — trace projection returns one trace per session from getSessionIds
buildFrame — trace projection assigns session colors by registration order from SESSION_COLORS
buildFrame — trace projection wraps color palette when session count exceeds palette length
buildFrame — trace projection each trace carries the session's arrived history for the requested metric
buildFrame — trace projection trace.latestValue equals the most recent arrived sample value
buildFrame — trace projection trace.latestValue is null when session has no history for the metric
buildFrame — trace projection excludes samples older than the 60-second window
buildFrame — frame metadata sets metric, yMax, unit, and now from METRICS config and argument
buildFrame — frame metadata has empty pulses array when store has no pulses
buildFrame — legend lists one legend entry per session with session color and latest value
buildFrame — legend reports null latestValue for sessions with no arrived history
buildFrame — pulse ordering by strength sorts three coexisting kinds as tool, subagent, lifecycle descending
buildFrame — pulse ordering by strength places higher-strength pulses before equal-or-lower-strength pulses
buildFrame — pulse ordering by strength is stable within the same strength: preserves relative insertion order
buildFrame — pulse vertical-position honesty pulse v equals the arrived rate value exactly at the pulse's time
buildFrame — pulse vertical-position honesty pulse v uses the most recent arrived value at-or-before the pulse time (no interpolation)
buildFrame — pulse vertical-position honesty pulse v is 0 when the session has no arrived rate history (baseline)
IC-S1: A 5-second tick of hook arrivals derives an events-per-second sample 15 events over 5 seconds yields a sample of 3 at the tick boundary
IC-S2: An OTel api-request event derives a tokens-per-second sample 500 tokens over 2 seconds yields a sample of 250
IC-S3: Tool-call events within a 5-second tick derive a tool-calls-per-second sample 10 tool calls over 5 seconds yields a sample of 2
IC-S4: A tool-use hook event emits a pulse at the event's timestamp pulse kind is tool and t matches the event time
IC-S5: A lifecycle hook event emits a pulse with a smaller strength than a tool-use pulse tool-use pulse strength exceeds lifecycle pulse strength
IC-S6: Appending rate samples preserves temporal order on read samples read back in oldest-to-newest order
IC-S7: Appending pulses preserves arrival order on read pulses read back in the arrival order they were appended
IC-S8: Querying a non-existent session returns empty history and pulses returns empty collections for a never-added session id
IC-S9: Subscribers are notified after appendRateSample subscriber callback is invoked exactly once
IC-S10: Subscribers are notified after appendPulse subscriber callback is invoked exactly once
IC-S10b: Subscribers are notified after addSession and removeSession addSession notifies once and removeSession notifies once — two total
IC-S11 @property: Frame values never invent sub-interval spikes beyond bracketing arrived values every projected trace value is bounded by its two bracketing arrived samples
IC-S12 @property: Frame values never zero-fill between arrivals edge value equals last arrived value and no sample zero-fills while the last arrival is non-zero
IC-S13 @property: Rate-sample append then read preserves timestamp order any in-order append sequence reads back in the same order with identical values
IC-S14 @property: Window trim is consistent across reads consecutive getRateHistory calls return arrays with identical contents
IC-S15 @property: Hit-test consistency between trace value and returned value selection value equals the selected trace's sampled value at selection.time within epsilon
IC-S16 @property: Pulse decay factor monotonically decreases with age decay at later age <= decay at earlier age, and pulses beyond lifetime are absent from the frame
scopeHitTest — within snap distance returns a HoverSelection when pointer is exactly on the sample
scopeHitTest — within snap distance returns a HoverSelection when pointer is vertically within HOVER_SNAP_DISTANCE_PX
scopeHitTest — within snap distance includes off-canvas (above yMax) traces — they clip to the top edge and remain hoverable
scopeHitTest — beyond snap distance returns null when the pointer is vertically farther than HOVER_SNAP_DISTANCE_PX from the trace
scopeHitTest — beyond snap distance property: pointer vertically farther than HOVER_SNAP_DISTANCE_PX from an on-scale trace returns null
scopeHitTest — pointer outside canvas bounds returns null when pointer x is negative
scopeHitTest — pointer outside canvas bounds returns null when pointer x exceeds width
scopeHitTest — pointer outside canvas bounds returns null when pointer y is negative
scopeHitTest — pointer outside canvas bounds returns null when pointer y exceeds height
scopeHitTest — empty frame returns null when frame has zero traces
scopeHitTest — empty frame returns null when all traces have zero samples
scopeHitTest — nearest of two candidates selects the trace whose sample is vertically closer to the pointer
scopeHitTest — nearest of two candidates does not select a trace whose sample is beyond snap when a closer trace is within snap
scopeHitTest — exported constants exports HOVER_SNAP_DISTANCE_PX = 28
M2-S1: A fresh tool call pulse flares brightest at arrival fresh pulse has decay factor at maximum value
M2-S2: A mid-life pulse carries a decay factor proportional to its age 1.25s-old pulse has decay factor of approximately 0.5
M2-S3: Pulse strength varies with event kind tool call pulse strength exceeds lifecycle pulse strength
M2-S3b: Three pulse kinds exhibit the full strength ordering tool > subagent > lifecycle — pulses sort by strength descending
M2-S4: Multiple pulses coexist on a single session's trace three pulses keep their own arrival times and decay factors
M2-S5: A pulse older than 2.5 seconds is absent from the frame 3s-old pulse is not projected into the frame
M2-S6: The store trims pulses older than the retention cutoff pulses older than 5s are absent from the store; younger pulses remain
M2-S7: A pulse references a session value from the same arrived history pulse's vertical value matches the arrived rate sample at its time
M2-S8: A session with pulses but no arrived rate history produces pulses at baseline pulse sits at zero baseline and no fabricated rate sample appears on the trace
M4-S1: Hover near a trace snaps to that session's nearest value selects session-1 at 12 evt/s with an age near 2 seconds
M4-S2: Hover snaps to the nearest of two overlapping traces selects the trace whose vertical position is closer to the pointer

M4-S3: Hover value comes from sampling the arrived history at the pointer's time value is the bracketing arrived sample at the pointer's time (no interpolation) and matches the trace's projected value
M4-S4: Hover beyond the snap threshold produces no selection returns null when vertical distance exceeds the snap threshold
M4-S5: Hover outside the scope area produces no selection returns null for pointer coordinates outside the scope rectangle
M4-S6: Hover with no active sessions produces no selection returns null when the frame has no traces
M4-S7: Hover at the right edge reports an age near zero age is less than 0.5 seconds at the current-time edge of the scope
M4-S8: Hover at the left edge reports an age near the window length age is approximately 60 seconds at the left edge of the scope
deriveEventsRate — reference example from IC-S1 15 events over a 5-second window yields 3 evt/s at the tick boundary
deriveEventsRate — zero events a count of zero yields a rate of zero
deriveEventsRate — fractional rate 12 events over a 5-second window yields a fractional 2.4 evt/s
deriveEventsRate — 1-second window boundary N events over a 1-second window yields N evt/s
deriveEventsRate — timestamp preservation (property) the returned t equals the input tickBoundaryT for any valid triplet
deriveEventsRate — linear scaling with count (property) doubling the count doubles the rate for a fixed window
deriveTokensRate — reference example from IC-S2 500 tokens over a 2-second duration yields 250 tok/s at the tick boundary
deriveTokensRate — zero-duration defensive lock a durationMs of zero yields a rate of zero (no throw)
deriveTokensRate — zero tokens a totalTokens of zero yields a rate of zero regardless of duration
deriveTokensRate — very-large totals 10 million tokens over a 1-second duration yields 10 million tok/s without overflow
deriveTokensRate — timestamp preservation (property) the returned t equals the input tickBoundaryT for any valid triplet
deriveToolCallsRate — reference example from IC-S3 10 tool calls over a 5-second window yields 2 calls/s at the tick boundary
deriveToolCallsRate — zero tool calls a count of zero yields a rate of zero
deriveToolCallsRate — fractional rate 3 tool calls over a 5-second window yields a fractional 0.6 calls/s
deriveToolCallsRate — timestamp preservation (property) the returned t equals the input tickBoundaryT for any valid triplet
deriveToolCallsRate — linear scaling with count (property) doubling the count doubles the rate for a fixed window
M3-S1: Default metric at first launch is Events per second first frame's metric is events and yMax is 15
M3-S2: Switching to Tokens per second re-projects with the tokens scale next frame uses tokens history with yMax 100 and tokens unit
M3-S3: Switching to Tool-calls per second re-projects with the tool-calls scale next frame uses tool-calls history with yMax 3 and tool-calls unit
M3-S4: Persistence buffer is reset at the metric-change boundary post-toggle frame uses the new metric's scale and carries no prior-metric sample values
M3-S5: Toggling back to the original metric re-projects from its own history events history is restored untouched after a round-trip through tokens
M3-S6: A session with history for one metric but not another projects an empty trace after toggle session appears but with empty samples and placeholder legend value
M3-S7: Hover is cleared when the metric changes the same pointer position yields a hover under the events scale but no hover under the tokens scale
WS-1: User glances at the scope and sees two sessions alive and churning projects one colored trace per active session with legend values
WS-2: User sees a fresh hook event flare as a pulse on its session's trace projects a pulse entry with age-based decay factor at the event's arrival time
WS-3: User switches the metric and the scope re-projects with the new scale the next frame uses tokens-per-second history and Y-axis maximum of 100
WS-4: User hovers over a trace and a tooltip identifies the session, value, and age hit-test returns session-2, value 47, age near 1.5 seconds
M5-S1: Adding a session makes it appear on the scope in the next frame second trace appears after addSession is called
M5-S2: Removing a session makes it disappear from the scope in the next frame middle session disappears from frame and legend after removeSession
M5-S3: The legend reflects the latest arrived value for each session legend entries carry the most recent arrived value per session
M5-S4: Ambient 60-second window excludes samples older than the window samples at 70s and 90s ago are not projected; 30s and 45s remain
M5-S5: Pulses older than their retention are absent from the store 6s-old pulse is not retained; pulses within 5s remain
M5-S6: Removing a session removes its associated rate buffers and pulses store holds no history or pulses for the removed session
M1-S1: Each active session gets its own color identity on the scope produces three traces with three distinct, stable session colors
M1-S2: A trace samples its session's arrived rate history across the window trace values stay bracketed by neighboring arrived samples (no sub-interval spikes)
M1-S3: A stalled session shows a flatline from the last arrived value, not a drop to zero flatlines at the last arrived value of 8 up to the current-time edge
M1-S4: A session with no arrived history yet produces an empty trace trace carries no samples and legend shows placeholder latest value
M1-S5: Samples older than the 60-second window are excluded from the trace no trace point uses a sample timestamped more than 60 seconds ago
M1-S6: Five concurrent sessions each receive a distinct color from the palette assigns each of the five palette colors exactly once across five sessions
M1-S7: No active sessions yields an empty scope with a clear legend frame contains zero traces and an empty legend; yMax reflects current metric
hookProcessor with token-bearing events hook prompt_submit with tokens does NOT credit totalTokens or sessionCost
hookProcessor with token-bearing events hook tool_call_end with tokens does NOT credit totalTokens or sessionCost
hookProcessor with token-bearing events OTel api_request with tokens is the only path that credits cost
hookProcessor with token-bearing events agent_complete decrements activeAgentCount without crediting tokens/cost
hookProcessor with non-token events tool_result increments toolCallCount without affecting tokens or cost
hookProcessor with non-token events tool_call_start (hook PreToolUse) is an identity signal under the OTel-first policy
hookProcessor with non-token events session_start increments activeAgentCount and sets sessionStartedAt
hookProcessor with non-token events unknown event type still increments totalEventCount
hookProcessor with DB event wrapper extracts usage from a nested api_request payload (production event format)
hookProcessor with DB event wrapper session_start wrapped event increments activeAgentCount
hookProcessor with malformed payloads null payload does not throw and increments totalEventCount
hookProcessor with malformed payloads undefined payload does not throw and increments totalEventCount
hookProcessor with malformed payloads payload missing event_type field treats as unknown event
hookProcessor pipeline accumulation multiple api_request events accumulate tokens and cost monotonically
hookProcessor pipeline accumulation hook and api_request events interleave: only api_request credits cost
hookProcessor pipeline accumulation mixed api_request, tool_result, and session_start events all increment totalEventCount
hookProcessor pipeline accumulation store updateMetrics is called once per processor invocation
hookProcessor: hook events never credit cost (OTel-authoritative policy) hook prompt_submit never credits cost, even with getIsOtelActive=false
hookProcessor: hook events never credit cost (OTel-authoritative policy) hook prompt_submit never credits cost, even with getIsOtelActive undefined
hookProcessor: mixed hook + OTel stream never double-counts cost hook + OTel for the same request: only the OTel cost is credited
hookProcessor: mixed hook + OTel stream never double-counts cost cost scaling is per api_request only, regardless of interleaved hooks
emitPulse — derives pulse strength from kind builds a tool-kind pulse with strength = PULSE_STRENGTHS.tool (1.0)
emitPulse — derives pulse strength from kind builds a subagent-kind pulse with strength = PULSE_STRENGTHS.subagent (0.75)
emitPulse — derives pulse strength from kind builds a lifecycle-kind pulse with strength = PULSE_STRENGTHS.lifecycle (0.5)
emitPulse — derives pulse strength from kind preserves the timestamp argument verbatim
multiSessionStore initial state has no sessions initially
multiSessionStore add and remove addSession registers a new session
multiSessionStore add and remove addSession with duplicate ID is idempotent
multiSessionStore add and remove removeSession removes tracked session
multiSessionStore add and remove removeSession on non-existent session is a no-op
multiSessionStore update and query updateSession replaces metrics for tracked session
multiSessionStore update and query getSession returns undefined for unknown session
multiSessionStore update and query tracks add and remove of sessions independently
multiSessionStore update and query updateSession on unknown ID is a no-op
multiSessionStore aggregate window buffer sums values across two sessions in the 1m window buffer
multiSessionStore aggregate window buffer does not aggregate non-aggregatable categories (context)
multiSessionStore buffer fallback paths getSessionWindowBuffer returns undefined for unknown session
multiSessionStore buffer fallback paths getSessionWindowBuffer returns empty buffer for session with no samples
multiSessionStore buffer fallback paths getAggregateWindowBuffer returns empty buffer for category with no samples
multiSessionStore buffer fallback paths getSessionBuffer returns undefined for unknown session
multiSessionStore buffer fallback paths appendSessionSample on unknown session is a no-op
Event pipeline integration: DB event → hookBridge → metricsStore OTel api_request with usage data produces non-zero tokens and cost in the store
Event pipeline integration: DB event → hookBridge → metricsStore hook tool_call_end refreshes context window but does not credit cost
Event pipeline integration: DB event → hookBridge → metricsStore session_start event increments activeAgentCount without adding tokens
Event pipeline integration: DB event → hookBridge → metricsStore OTel tool_result increments toolCallCount without adding tokens
Event pipeline integration: DB event → hookBridge → metricsStore hook prompt_submit with usage refreshes context without adding tokens/cost
Event pipeline integration: DB event → hookBridge → metricsStore time series buffer receives samples when api_request events flow
